etymology.description: 太(太) (tài, "too; very; excessively") + 好(好) (hǎo, "good; well") + 了(了) (le, "particle indicating change of state") = expressing very positive excitement when something turns good -> great; fantastic
etymology.interpretation: 太(太) (tài, "too; very") conveys a sense of excess or extremity, leading to an amplified form of 'good'. When combined with 好(好) (hǎo, "good"), it intensifies the quality of being 'good' to a higher degree. The particle 了(了) (le, "change of state") marks the speaker's realization or acknowledgment of this enhanced state of quality, making the expression one of exclamation and enthusiasm akin to 'great' or 'fantastic'.

examples.chinese: 你通过考试了(你通過考試了)，太好了(太好了)！
examples.pinyin: Nǐ tōngguò kǎoshì le, tài hǎo le!
examples.english: You passed the exam, that's great!

examples.chinese: 我们今天去海边(我們今天去海邊)，太好了(太好了)！
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jīntiān qù hǎibiān, tài hǎo le!
examples.english: We are going to the beach today, fantastic!

examples.chinese: 他们接受了你的提案(他們接受了你的提案)，太好了(太好了)！
examples.pinyin: Tāmen jiēshòu le nǐ de tí'àn, tài hǎo le!
examples.english: They accepted your proposal, that's great!
